next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 18

The memorandum called it a change in supervisory circumstances.

Leela read the phrase three times because the first two had not felt insulting enough. The PDF sat open on her laptop beside the stripped-down dissertation, both windows full of tracked changes and institutional blue headers, both pretending language could make scale disappear. Change in supervisory circumstances. As if Mohit had taken a sabbatical. As if he had developed a polite rash and not spent a year turning his students into weapons against one another, then funneled their work into a startup deck with his soft little mentor's smile still warm on it.

Her studio was hot in the stale, boxed-in way graduate housing got hot once the radiators finally shut up and the building remembered it had no idea what to do with summer. The cheap blinds clicked against the frame. A fan pushed the air from one side of the room to the other without improving it. On the mattress behind her sat three stacks of papers she kept meaning to sort: committee forms, credit-card statements, and the dissertation drafts from before her project had been reduced to something survivable.

The current file was one hundred sixty-eight pages if she counted the bibliography and the appendix she no longer respected. In January it had been two hundred forty-two. The missing pages had not vanished cleanly. She had cut away the benchmark chapter whose figures had shown up, translated and polished, in `ruderal_pitch_v3.pdf`. She had cut the section with the robustness result that had not replicated cleanly and had looked so beauti...
open_hooks_to_carry: Stage the open house as a polished institutional performance whose surfaces crack under documentary fact.
Ensure the reveal happens through contradictory artifacts, witness alignment, and timing rather than through a villain confession.
prev_tail_excerpt:
...ts ever became public. That version did not exist. It had not existed the night she hid the logs, or the deadline week she let the false result stand, or the hour she doctored Omar's letter because the clean procedure was a polite machine for immigration disaster. Wanting a clean alibi had been one more way of staying loyal to the institution's taste in victims.

Marisol labeled a folder HOLDOUT SET / L.K. / MARCH 2026 and slid it into a drawer. "Leela," she said, looking up for the first time in several minutes, "they can already call you compromised. He already did. The committee already stamped it. The difference is whether compromised means documented and contextualized or whether it means whatever version he tells first."

When Leela stood, the room tilted from hunger. Marisol pointed to a basket of granola bars without looking up from her notes. "Take one. Counsel's not your mother, but counsel can still tell when somebody hasn't eaten."

Leela took one because refusing would have felt theatrical. It was peanut butter and stale at the edges. By the time she got downstairs the snow had started again, not dramatic flakes, just the dry mean kind that moved sideways under the streetlights. Her backpack was lighter by almost nothing. The difference was elsewhere. The archive now lived on more than one machine, under a name she had chosen for the part of the year that had not been allowed to disappear into training data, committee prose, or her own wish to still look good in the retelling.

Whatever story Haviland later told, `holdout_set` no longer depended on Leela alone.
state_deltas_required: The former lab members coordinate a release of evidence during Haviland's industry demo day so Mohit cannot easily contain the story.
June backs the evidence, Mohit is placed on administrative leave, and Ruderal's deal freezes, but the lab's publication pipeline and recommendation network collapse with him.
